Description: S3 event notification configurations — trigger Lambda, SQS, or SNS on object events

Example1_LambdaTriggerOnUpload.Description: Trigger a Lambda function when any object is uploaded (created) — most common pattern for image processing, thumbnails, etc.
Example1_LambdaTriggerOnUpload.LambdaPermissionNeeded: aws lambda add-permission --function-name ProcessImage --principal s3.amazonaws.com --action lambda:InvokeFunction --source-arn arn:aws:s3:::my-bucket --statement-id S3InvokePermission

Example2_SQSOnDelete.Description: Send a message to SQS when an object is deleted — useful for audit trails and cascading deletes

Example3_SNSFanOut.Description: Publish to SNS topic on any object event — fans out to multiple subscribers (Lambda, SQS, email)

Example4_EventBridgeIntegration.Description: Send ALL S3 events to EventBridge for advanced filtering and routing — more flexible than native S3 notifications
Example4_EventBridgeIntegration.WhyEventBridge: EventBridge supports content-based filtering (object size, metadata), can route to 20+ target types, and supports archive/replay. Use EventBridge over native S3 notifications when you need complex routing.

EventTypes.s3:ObjectCreated:*: Any object creation (Put, Post, Copy, CompleteMultipartUpload)
EventTypes.s3:IntelligentTiering: Object moved between Intelligent-Tiering access tiers

InterviewTip: S3 event notifications are the backbone of event-driven architectures. Key points: (1) Native notifications go to Lambda, SQS, or SNS directly. (2) EventBridge gives more flexibility (content filtering, 20+ targets). (3) You can only have ONE notification configuration per bucket (but it can have multiple rules). (4) Notifications are best-effort — for guaranteed delivery, use EventBridge with DLQ.
